title: hiraea polyantha
intro: Hiraea polyantha, also known as the Red Ironwood or Rengarengá in the Maori language, is a small, shrub-like tree native to New Zealand. It has been used for centuries in traditional Maori medicine, valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Hiraea polyantha include its ability to soothe skin conditions, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Hiraea polyantha can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be done under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to the potential for allergic reactions and interactions with other medications.
